DEMOCRATS GAIN IN NORTH DAKOTA, BUT G.O.P. LEADS; Farm Unrest Aids Stevenson, Survey Shows--Cut Seen in Vote for Eisenhower State Going Two-Party Survey Indicates Farm Unrest in North Dakota Reduces G.O.P. Lead CUT IN VOTE SEEN FOR EISENHOWER State Swinging to 2-Party System--Langer, Bumper Crops Help President Price-Cost Squeeze Mayor Going Democratic. Confidence in Eisenhower Voters Decide to Switch Families Split Politically
Times survey of ND indicates Dems gain because of farm unrest but Repub defeat is unlikely; finds no Repub leaders think Pres will run as strongly as '52; 2d major factor for Dem gains is shift of Non-Partisan League from Repubs to Dems; Sen Langer support of Eisenhower seen factor; survey finds Repubs will continue to dominate urban vote; finds some opposition to Stevenson because of his divorce
